notes: DISTINCT META POLICIES (151 files, 6 policies — confirmed via `sort|uniq -c` on the exact meta line, i.e. byte-identical within groups): [A] "marketing-full" — 137 pages: 131 byte-identical lines (estimate.html, privacy.html, review.html + 128 docs/services/*.html flat templates: hail-damage-*, gutter-replacement-*, roof-*, financing, fire-water-smoke, etc.) + 6 pages with the identical policy but a trailing viewport meta on the same physical line (about.html, our-work.html + 4 service pages). Actual loads verified on representatives (estimate.html deep, hail-damage-cincinnati-oh.html, about, our-work, privacy, review): Google Fonts CSS (style-src fonts.googleapis OK) + woff2 (font-src fonts.gstatic OK); gtag G-8PG7N9Q3DL (script googletagmanager OK, beacons connect www.google-analytics.com OK); local scripts only on templates (nav-faq.js, inline/479bd49556 hamburger, inline/b1d1695580, ann-bar.js — zero network calls, grep-verified). estimate.html extras all verified allowed in BOTH meta and header: firebasejs 10.12.2 module imports from www.gstatic.com (script-src), unpkg Leaflet js+css (script/style-src), nominatim.openstreetmap.org autocomplete fetch (connect), maps.googleapis.com staticmap as (meta https:, header maps.googleapis.com), arcgisonline satellite tiles as (header *.arcgisonline.com), us-central1 cloudfunctions (saveFunnelProgress fetch, submitPublicLead, httpsCallable sendVerificationCode/verifyCode OTP via *.cloudfunctions.net + *.googleapis.com). Leaflet pin is an inline divIcon (no unpkg marker PNGs — no img gap). The ONE real break is the workers.dev AI proxy (finding 1). Meta A's api.resend.com + js.stripe + jsdelivr/cdnjs grants are unused by anything these pages load (over-broad, harmless). All 6 metas carry script-src 'unsafe-inline' which the header nullifies — misleading but not itself breaking (pages were already extracted). [B] "minimal GTM/GA" — 10 pages: blog/{the-pipe-boot-fork, why-i-install-lumanail-on-every-elite-roof, why-roofivent-is-on-my-roofs}.html, the-pledge/index.html, services/{gaf-pivot-boot,gaf-timberline,lumanail,roofivent,the-nbd-build,the-nbd-guarantee}/index.html. Loads: fonts + gtag + local-only scripts (nav-faq, 479bd49556, b833f00105/a83ef8e79e, ann-bar, blog-midpost-cta — all grep-clean of fetch/XHR/external src). connect-src lacks *.cloudfunctions.net but nothing on these pages calls it; no iframes; img all local. NO CONFLICTS. (Caveat for future: dropping public-lead-submit.js onto any of these templates will be connect-src-blocked by this meta.) [C] inspect.html — minimal + *.cloudfunctions.net, img-src without http:. Loads gtag, fonts, public-lead-submit.js → POST submitPublicLead (allowed), inspect-form.js (UTM passthrough, no other net). NO current conflict; Turnstile latent gap shared with finding 2 (script-src and frame-src would block it). [D] free-roof/index.html — Turnstile-complete policy (script/connect/frame all include challenges.cloudflare.com + pinned us-central1-nobigdeal-pro.cloudfunctions.net). Loads fonts + public-lead-submit + inline/0a394536a8 (no net). No GTM tag on this page at all (script-src grant unused — analytics gap, not CSP). NO CONFLICTS — this is the model policy the lead pages should copy. [E] index.html — policy A + formsubmit.co in connect-src. VERIFIED the known fix: formsubmit.co present in meta (docs/index.html:5) AND header (firebase.json:60 connect-src) — not re-reported. Contact form has dual path: inline/72f02d79d0.js → fetch formsubmit.co (allowed) and inline/0214ed5eea.js → submitPublicLead('contact') (allowed). All other index scripts grep-clean. [F] visualizer.html — policy A + recaptcha paths (www.google.com/recaptcha/, www.gstatic.com/recaptcha/ in script-src; www.google.com/recaptcha/ in frame-src), workers.dev REMOVED from connect-src. Module inline/b9b56a8331.module.js imports firebase-app + firebase-app-check from gstatic (allowed) but only initializes App Check if window.__NBD_RECAPTCHA_KEY__ is set — grep shows NOTHING sets it anywhere in docs/, so the recaptcha grants are currently inert (and App Check is never attached; if publicVisualizerAI/visualizerImageGen ever enforce App Check the page breaks for a non-CSP reason — worth knowing). Main script inline/27bd7bf65b.js fetches us-central1 cloudfunctions /publicVisualizerAI + /visualizerImageGen (connect allowed both layers); canvas/preview images are data:/blob: (img-src allows in meta and header). NO current conflicts. HEADER MODEL: only the global `**` rule (firebase.json:60) applies to all the above pages; the /pro/* route-specific CSPs (lines 104-196) never match docs marketing paths — confirmed via the source patterns at firebase.json headers block. COULD NOT CHECK: docs/pro/terms.html does not exist on origin/main (it lives on unmerged PR #579 branch feat/pro-terms-page) — listed as high-interest but unauditable here. Live-browser verification of the workers.dev block was not performed (read-only repo audit), but repo == deploy source (auto-deploy on main) so the header analysis reflects prod. GA4 note: only the primary www.google-analytics.com endpoint is allowed (both layers); regional endpoints (region1.google-analytics.com) and doubleclick signals would be blocked by both meta and header if Google ever routes beacons there — informational only, primary endpoint works.

Scope: NEW-D26 audit (latent undeclared/unprovided identifiers) of 10 standalone pages in C:/Users/jonat/nbd-wt-audit (origin/main @ d1608acb). Method: built each page's full script list from its <script> tags, read every page-specific script in full (portal.js 1531 lines, vault-page.js key sections + full dispatch tables, app.js boot/delegate/theme sections), and ran a custom regex-based undeclared-identifier scanner (node, strings/comments/regex stripped, declarations incl. params/destructuring/window-assigns collected, provider files cross-checked) over all 20+ scripts. Verified clean — no genuinely dangling identifiers: estimate-view.js, refer.js, demo-reveal.js, sign-page.js, how-to.js, pages/login.js, pages/register.js, pages/photo-review.js (identifier-level), portal.js (beyond known-fixed TOKEN/repName; window.NBDBeforeAfter is provided by before-after-slider.js which portal.html loads first, both defer, plus a setTimeout retry guard), vault-auth.module.js, login.html's shared scripts (login-reset/standalone-compat/theme-init/offline-manager/sentry-init), and all four daily-success scripts. Cross-script contracts verified OK: ds-firebase-sync.js's window.savePages/loadPages/renderTabs/renderDash exist because app.js is a classic top-level script (its only IIFEs are NBD_USER_PLAN at 1154-1168, nbdBoot, nbdWireDelegates); every data-action/data-input-action value in daily-success/index.html maps to a declared handler; Chart (app.js:563) comes from the cdnjs CDN tag which the global ** CSP allows (daily-success has no route override); app.js:1207's bare currentUser/db reads are typeof-guarded; vault-page.js's click-dispatch noArg table (line 182) — which evaluates 10 bare identifiers on EVERY data-v-action click — was checked name-by-name, all declared; vault.html's static data-v-action values (manualSave/navTo/startNextSession) and all dynamically-rendered ones resolve; all getElementById targets used by login/register/sign/how-to/photo-review/portal scripts exist in their HTML. Element-ID and DCL-timing checks done (register.js's bare DOMContentLoaded wiring is safe: parser-inserted modules with top-level await delay DCL). Limits: the scanner treats function params as file-wide declarations, so scope-shadowing false negatives are possible in the two largest files (vault-page.js 2311 lines, app.js 1492 lines) — mitigated by manual reads of all interaction handlers and window.* greps; I could not execute pages in a browser (static audit only), so the vault firebase-ready finding is inferred from exhaustive repo-wide greps (no dispatcher, no _firestore producer) plus git history showing the same gap pre-extraction. daily-success/index.html references /pro/daily-success/ds-firebase-sync.js (repo root of that folder, not js/) — file exists there, not a 404.

Scope covered: docs/pro/estimate-view.html (+js/estimate-view.js), sign.html (+js/sign-page.js), photo-review.html (+js/pages/photo-review.js module, js/portal-link-helpers.js, js/nbd-emulator-connect.js existence), vault.html (+js/vault-auth.module.js, js/vault-page.js, js/nbd-auth.js as imported dep), daily-success/index.html (+js/auth-init.js, js/welcome-gate.js, js/app.js, ds-firebase-sync.js, CDN Chart.js). Method: enumerated every data-*-action / data-on-* / data-input-action / inline on*= attribute and control-looking id in BOTH static HTML and renderer template strings, then verified a matching listener/delegate entry AND a declared handler function exists in a script actually loaded on that page; also reverse-checked getElementById/querySelector targets against each page's DOM (all resolve — vault admin/search/session ids are emitted by renderAdmin/renderSearch/renderSessions templates; daily-success gpi-*/c-* ids are emitted by renderDash). Verified the global ** CSP (firebase.json:60) carries script-src-attr 'none' (kills inline on*= attrs) and script-src without unsafe-inline. CLEAN: sign.html fully wired (spSubmit listener sign-page.js:134, all 7 sp* ids present); daily-success delegates complete — all 27 static + ~20 renderer-emitted data-action/data-input-action values have dispatch entries (app.js:1413-1465) and every referenced function is declared; ds-firebase-sync's window.savePages/loadPages/renderTabs/renderDash interception targets are real top-level globals; photo-review's main wiring (filter bar, chip tap/long-press, bulk bar incl. data-bulk-field, picker, lightbox, lasso, toast, prGenerateBtn→customer.html#photo-report which customer.html:8224 handles) all bound; vault's click delegate covers all 14 data-v-action values (HTML + emitted) and all dispatch-table identifiers are declared (no latent ReferenceError in the noArg table). Could NOT runtime-verify (static audit only, read-only worktree): actual Firestore rules acceptance of photo-review's photos writes (rules-denied class — other agent), and live CSP behavior (inferred from header text). Side observations for other audit classes: docs/admin/vault.html still carries large inline <script> blocks incl. the firebase-ready dispatcher — under the ** CSP those are refused, so the admin copy of the vault is likely NEW-D23-dead too; docs/pro/vault.html has a malformed duplicated </body></html> before its vault-page.js script tag (parser-tolerated, script still executes — cosmetic); photo-review.html and ds-firebase-sync.js embed two different apiKey/appId firebaseConfig blobs than the canonical one in nbd-auth.js (messagingSenderId 555556015293 vs 717435841570) — worth confirming the photo-review one is a real key, since a wrong appId/apiKey would break auth on that page at runtime (NEW-D26-adjacent, not verifiable statically).

Scope: read firestore.rules (839 lines) and storage.rules (182 lines) in full, then enumerated every Firestore write/query and Storage upload in all 11 listed scripts at C:/Users/jonat/nbd-wt-audit. Per-file disposition: (1) estimate-view.js, refer.js, portal.js, sign-page.js — homeowner/token pages with NO Firebase auth; verified they make ZERO direct Firestore/Storage SDK calls (grep clean) — everything routes through token-validated Cloud Functions (getEstimateForView, submitReferral, getHomeownerPortalView, recordCustomerEvent, getPortalMessages, sendPortalMessage, submitCustomerRating, requestCallback, uploadHomeownerPhoto, getSignDocument, submitSignature), which is the correct pattern; no public rules needed. (2) pages/photo-review.js (authed rep page): leads getDoc is owner-readable (firestore.rules:74); photos onSnapshot query includes BOTH where('leadId'==) and the required where('userId','==',uid) owner clause satisfying rules:284; updateDoc/batch.update/batch.delete on photos are owner-allowed (rules:284-285) — all operations possible. (3) pages/login.js: auth + httpsCallable only, no Firestore. (4) pages/register.js: setDoc users/{uid} create payload {firstName,lastName,company,email,createdAt,onboarded} contains none of the banned keys ['role','plan','accessCode','isAdmin','companyId'] → allowed by rules:338-339; googleRegister getDoc/setDoc same. (5) ds-firebase-sync.js: users/{uid}/ds_pages and ds_meta writes are allowed via the users/{uid}/{subcol}/{docId} wildcard (rules:344-346) and pullFromFirestore's getDocs on the owner's own subcollection is allowed — ONLY the leaderboard write is denied, but it atomically kills the whole batch (finding 1). (6) vault-page.js + vault-auth.module.js: finding 2. (7) daily-success/js/app.js: finding 3 (guarded dead path, rules would allow). Storage: none of the 11 files contains uploadBytes/uploadString/.put()/getStorage — storage.rules not implicated by this scope. Known-fixed items (reports owner-delete, deal_rooms isHtmlOnly, estimates userId stamp, loadReports userId clause, academy_progress/deal_rooms blocks) not re-reported. Incidental observations not filed as findings: (a) the daily_entries/{uid}/entries rule at firestore.rules:476-478 is orphaned — no client code in docs/ touches that collection (daily-success uses users/{uid}/ds_pages instead; only functions/integrations/compliance.js references it for erasure); (b) leads read rule (rules:74) grants no manager/company-scope read despite the role-taxonomy comment at rules:23 promising managers 'read all team leads' — none of the 11 scoped scripts hits this, so it was out of scope here but may matter for team dashboards; (c) photo-review.js and ds-firebase-sync.js embed different appId/messagingSenderId Firebase configs (555556015293 vs 717435841570) — both same project, cosmetic. Could not runtime-verify denials (read-only audit, no emulator run), but every claim is grounded in exact rule lines plus batch-atomicity semantics.

Coverage: read every byte of estimate-view.html+js, photo-review.html+js+portal-link-helpers.js, sign.html+sign-page.js, vault.html+vault-auth.module.js+vault-page.js (all 2311 lines), daily-success index.html (body/controls regions; the 700-line CSS head skimmed via grep) + app.js (all 1492 lines) + auth-init.js + welcome-gate.js + ds-firebase-sync.js. Cross-checked gates against firebase.json global ** CSP (script-src-attr 'none', no unsafe-inline; connect-src allows *.cloudfunctions.net but NOT api.anthropic.com or the workers.dev proxy), firestore.rules (photos owner rule :283, users subcollections :344, leaderboard write:false :395, NO codex/codex-sessions match → default deny :834), and the two token mint flows (createPortalToken onCall functions/portal.js:131 → portal_tokens used by estimate-view; createSignRequest onCall functions/remote-signing.js:67 → doc_sign_tokens used by sign.html, emailed, 7-day TTL, burned on submit). None of the five pages ships its own <meta http-equiv=CSP> (NEW-D27 class: zero hits on these surfaces). What I could NOT verify statically: runtime behavior (which gates actually fire live), the contents of signature-widget.js injected into sign.html's srcdoc (lives in the doc-generator pipeline, out of the five-surface scope), nbd-auth.js's full redirect matrix for the vault professional+admin gate, and whether /assets/css/nbd-mobile.css (referenced by vault + daily-success) exists. Per instructions this is inventory-only; CSP-dead inline handlers (vault's two oninput= search inputs), never-dispatched firebase-ready on /pro/vault, rules-denied codex + leaderboard writes, the photo-review Share copy-step missing CustomerPortal/global-Firestore deps, and the vault eval-on-paste are recorded as gate/conditional facts inside the relevant control-group entries rather than as separate bug findings.
